text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
GASTROINTESTINAL AND HEPATOBILIARY PATHOLOGY DIVISION

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED], MD (Hepatobiliary Surgical Oncology)
REFERRING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED], MD (Medical Oncology)
CLINICAL SERVICE: Surgical Oncology
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

CLINICAL HISTORY:
66-year-old female with known history of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), pancreatic head and uncinate process, initially diagnosed as borderline resectable (T4 N1 M0, AJCC 8th edition). Patient received neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles, with dose modifications for peripheral neuropathy) followed by consolidative long-course chemoradiation (50.4 Gy in 28 fractions with concurrent capecitabine). Post-neoadjuvant restaging demonstrated partial radiographic response with tumor size reduction from 3.8 cm to 2.3 cm and CA 19-9 decline from 1,247 U/mL to 72 U/mL. Multidisciplinary tumor board determined tumor was amenable to resection. Intraoperative frozen section margins reported as negative. Specimen submitted for permanent section analysis.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, head and uncinate process, post-neoadjuvant therapy.

OPERATION PERFORMED:
Pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure)

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
DATE RECEIVED IN PATHOLOGY: [TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
DATE OF SIGN-OUT: [TO BE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
A. Pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen (en bloc): pancreatic head and uncinate process, duodenum, gallbladder, distal common bile duct, and proximal jejunum (approximately 15 cm).
B. Pancreatic neck margin (intraoperative frozen section remnant, permanent).
C. Common bile duct margin (intraoperative frozen section remnant, permanent).
D. Peripancreatic lymph nodes, additional (submitted separately by surgeon).

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A. PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY SPECIMEN:

1. PANCREAS, HEAD AND UNCINATE PROCESS — PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA, POST-NEOADJUVANT THERAPY:
   - Histologic type: Infiltrating ductal adenocarcinoma (pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, NOS)
   - Histologic grade: Moderately differentiated (grade 2 of 3)
   - Tumor size (macroscopic): 2.1 cm x 1.8 cm x 1.5 cm in greatest dimensions
     (Note: Extensive treatment-related fibrosis and desmoplastic stroma with residual viable tumor nests; the full extent of original tumor bed estimated at approximately 3.0 x 2.5 cm by examination of grossly fibrotic region)
   - Treatment effect: PRESENT — MODERATE RESPONSE
     College of American Pathologists (CAP) Tumor Regression Score: 2
     (Moderate response: Residual cancer with evident tumor regression, but more than single cells or rare small groups of cancer cells)
     Estimated percentage of residual viable carcinoma: approximately 25-35% of original tumor bed
     Remaining tumor bed composed of: dense paucicellular fibrosis with hemosiderin-laden macrophages, ghost cell outlines, acellular mucin pools (focal), cholesterol clefts, foamy histiocytes, and scattered inflammatory infiltrate — all consistent with treatment-related necrosis and fibrosis
   - Tumor location: Pancreatic head and uncinate process with involvement of periampullary tissue
   - Ampulla of Vater: Not involved by carcinoma; ampullary mucosa intact
   - Duodenum: Not involved by carcinoma

2. MARGINS (all margins assessed on permanent sections):
   - Pancreatic neck (transection) margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
     Distance from carcinoma to margin: 0.4 cm
     (Confirmed on permanent sections; concordant with intraoperative frozen section diagnosis of no carcinoma)
   - Common bile duct margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
     Distance from carcinoma to margin: 0.8 cm
     (Confirmed on permanent sections; concordant with intraoperative frozen section)
   - Posterior (retroperitoneal / SMA) margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
     Distance from carcinoma to margin: 0.1 cm (CLOSE MARGIN — see comment)
   - Anterior pancreatic surface: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
     Distance from carcinoma to margin: 0.3 cm
   - Uncinate process margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
     Distance from carcinoma to margin: 0.3 cm
   - Proximal jejunal margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
   - Distal gastric/duodenal margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
   COMMENT (posterior/SMA margin): The posterior retroperitoneal margin is 0.1 cm (1 mm) from the nearest viable residual tumor focus. Although this margin is negative (no carcinoma at the inked surface), it is considered a close margin. In the post-neoadjuvant context, areas of dense fibrosis at the SMA margin show treatment effect without definitive viable tumor cells at the margin ink. The margin is therefore classified as R0 (microscopically negative). Correlation with preoperative imaging and intraoperative findings is recommended.

3. LYMPH NODES:
   - Total lymph nodes examined: 22
   - Lymph nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma: 0 of 22 (ypN0)
   - Largest lymph node: 1.1 cm (station: peripancreatic; shows reactive changes with fibrosis, no viable tumor)
   - Lymph nodes examined by station:
       Peripancreatic (anterior): 0/5 — negative (treatment effect/fibrosis in 2 nodes)
       Peripancreatic (posterior): 0/4 — negative
       Along hepatic artery: 0/4 — negative
       Periportal/hilar: 0/3 — negative
       Peripancreatic (inferior): 0/3 — negative
       Additional submitted separately (Specimen D): 0/3 — negative
   - Note: Two peripancreatic lymph nodes demonstrate florid fibrosis and hemosiderin deposition with foamy histiocyte aggregates, consistent with prior lymph node metastasis with complete treatment-induced regression (pathologic complete nodal response). No viable carcinoma identified in these nodes.

4. LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: PRESENT (focal)
   - Lymphatic invasion: Present in a single focus adjacent to the main tumor mass; identified on H&E with confirmation on D2-40 (podoplanin) immunostain — see ancillary studies below
   - Venous (large vessel) invasion: Not identified

5. PERINEURAL INVASION: PRESENT
   - Perineural invasion identified in 3 separate foci within and immediately adjacent to the residual viable carcinoma
   - No involvement of named neural structures identified

6. PATHOLOGIC STAGING (Post-Neoadjuvant, AJCC 8th Edition, TNM):
   - Primary tumor (ypT): ypT2 (tumor limited to pancreas, >2 cm but ≤4 cm; residual viable tumor measures 2.1 cm)
   - Regional lymph nodes (ypN): ypN0 (0/22 lymph nodes positive)
   - Distant metastasis (ypM): Not applicable (not assessed in this specimen)
   - Overall pathologic stage: ypT2 ypN0 Mx — Stage IIA (post-neoadjuvant)
   - Resection status: R0 (all margins microscopically negative)

7. ADDITIONAL FINDINGS IN PANCREAS:
   - Pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN): PanIN grade 1 and PanIN grade 2 present in background pancreatic parenchyma remote from the primary tumor
   - Background pancreatic parenchyma: Extensive chronic pancreatitis with acinar atrophy, fibrosis, and islet cell aggregation — consistent with obstructive pancreatitis from prior ductal obstruction
   - No evidence of additional synchronous carcinoma

8. GALLBLADDER: No carcinoma. Chronic cholecystitis with mild mucosal atrophy. No cholesterol polyps. No cholelithiasis identified on gross examination.

9. DUODENUM: No carcinoma. Mild villous blunting of duodenal mucosa, likely treatment-related. No peptic ulceration. No polyps.

10. DISTAL COMMON BILE DUCT: Mild reactive biliary epithelial changes. No carcinoma. No high-grade dysplasia (bile duct intraepithelial neoplasia).

11. PROXIMAL JEJUNUM: No carcinoma. Normal small intestinal mucosa.

B. PANCREATIC NECK MARGIN (Intraoperative Frozen Section Permanent):
   - NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
   - Exocrine pancreatic tissue with chronic pancreatitis changes. No carcinoma. No PanIN of high grade.
   - Concordant with intraoperative frozen section diagnosis.

C. COMMON BILE DUCT MARGIN (Intraoperative Frozen Section Permanent):
   - NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
   - Bile duct wall with reactive mucosal changes. No carcinoma. No high-grade biliary intraepithelial neoplasia.
   - Concordant with intraoperative frozen section diagnosis.

D. PERIPANCREATIC LYMPH NODES (Additional, Separately Submitted):
   - 3 lymph nodes examined, 0/3 positive
   - All negative for metastatic carcinoma
   - Reactive lymphoid hyperplasia

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SPECIMEN A — PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY:
Received fresh and then fixed in 10% neutral buffered formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number. The specimen consists of a pancreaticoduodenectomy (pylorus-preserving) including: the pancreatic head and uncinate process measuring 7.5 x 5.0 x 4.0 cm, the duodenum measuring 25 cm in length, the gallbladder measuring 9.0 x 3.5 cm, and the distal common bile duct measuring 3.0 cm in length. The proximal jejunum measures approximately 15 cm. The specimen is oriented and inked per standard protocol: posterior/retroperitoneal (SMA) margin in black, anterior surface in blue, uncinate margin in green, and the transected pancreatic neck in red.

Gross examination of the pancreatic head reveals a poorly circumscribed, firm, whitish-yellow fibrotic lesion in the head and uncinate process measuring 3.0 x 2.5 cm in estimated tumor bed (based on fibrotic region). Within the fibrotic area, there are foci of residual tan-white firm tissue measuring 2.1 x 1.8 cm. The fibrotic region extends to within 1 mm of the posteriorly inked (SMA) margin at its closest point. The pancreatic neck margin is submitted as a perpendicular section. The main pancreatic duct, when serially sectioned, is dilated up to 4 mm (improved from 6 mm at diagnosis) and opens near the ampulla of Vater, which appears unremarkable. No stones are identified in the pancreatic duct or bile duct.

The common bile duct lumen is patent. The gallbladder mucosa is tan-green with a fine granular surface; no gallstones or intraluminal lesions are identified. The duodenal mucosa appears intact without ulceration or mass lesion. The proximal jejunal segment is unremarkable.

Twenty-two peripancreatic and regional lymph nodes are identified in the peripancreatic adipose tissue, ranging from 0.3 cm to 1.1 cm. Several show firm, white-gray cut surfaces consistent with fibrosis. Representative sections submitted entirely per protocol.

Representative sections submitted: sections through the posterior margin at the closest point to the tumor, perpendicular pancreatic neck margin, sections through the tumor bed (at least 12 blocks through the main fibrotic/tumor region), ampulla of Vater, proximal and distal bile duct margins, gallbladder (fundus, body, cystic duct margin), duodenal mucosa, proximal jejunal margin, and all lymph nodes in their entirety.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on paraffin-embedded sections of residual viable tumor):

1. CK7 (cytokeratin 7): POSITIVE — diffuse, strong cytoplasmic positivity in residual tumor cells; consistent with pancreatobiliary origin
2. CK20 (cytokeratin 20): NEGATIVE — no staining in tumor cells
3. CDX2: NEGATIVE — no nuclear staining in tumor cells
4. MUC1: POSITIVE — luminal/apical membrane positivity in neoplastic glands
5. MUC2: NEGATIVE
6. SMAD4 (DPC4): LOSS OF EXPRESSION — residual tumor cells show complete absence of SMAD4 nuclear and cytoplasmic protein expression (internal positive control: pancreatic islets and adjacent normal acini stain strongly positive); findings consistent with homozygous SMAD4 deletion, concordant with prior biopsy IHC result
7. p53: ABERRANT PATTERN — overexpression (diffuse, strong nuclear positivity) in residual tumor cells; consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation (concordant with prior genomic profiling showing TP53 p.R248W)
8. D2-40 (podoplanin): POSITIVE — confirms lymphovascular invasion in one focus at tumor periphery
9. Ki-67 (MIB-1): Approximately 20% of residual viable tumor cells show nuclear positivity (decreased from 35% on pre-treatment biopsy, consistent with treatment effect)
10. MLH1: RETAINED nuclear expression
11. MSH2: RETAINED nuclear expression
12. MSH6: RETAINED nuclear expression
13. PMS2: RETAINED nuclear expression
   — Interpretation: No loss of mismatch repair protein expression. Results consistent with microsatellite-stable (MSS) status, concordant with prior molecular testing.

SPECIAL STAINS:
- Mucicarmine: Focal intraluminal mucin in residual tumor glands; also highlights acellular mucin pools in treatment-effect areas
- Masson's trichrome: Extensive collagen deposition in treatment-effect fibrosis; highlights the desmoplastic stroma surrounding residual tumor nests

ADDITIONAL ANCILLARY STUDIES — PENDING:
- KRAS mutation analysis by PCR/pyrosequencing on the resection specimen (ordered for quality confirmation of primary tumor identity; result pending at time of sign-out)

Note: No additional molecular/genomic profiling has been ordered on the resection specimen at this time, as comprehensive genomic profiling was previously performed on the diagnostic biopsy specimen and is on file.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Multiple sections through the pancreatic head and uncinate process demonstrate a moderately differentiated infiltrating ductal adenocarcinoma with extensive treatment-related changes. Residual viable carcinoma is present in the form of irregular, angulated glands and small nests of epithelial cells embedded within a densely fibrotic, paucicellular desmoplastic stroma. The neoplastic glands exhibit moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, irregular luminal contours, and intraluminal dirty necrosis in scattered foci. Mitotic figures are infrequent (1-2 per 10 high-power fields) in areas of residual viable tumor.

Surrounding the residual viable tumor, extensive treatment effect is identified: large zones of acellular hyalinized fibrosis, hemosiderin-laden macrophages, foamy histiocytes, cholesterol cleft foreign body giant cell reaction, scattered lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate, and focal acellular mucin pools. Ghost outlines of necrotic glands are present. These findings account for approximately 65-75% of the original estimated tumor bed and are consistent with substantial but incomplete tumor regression, designated CAP Tumor Regression Score 2.

Perineural invasion is present, with carcinoma cells identified encircling and permeating small to medium-sized nerves in 3 separate foci within the tumor and at the periphery of the treatment-effect zone. Lymphovascular invasion is present in one focus at the leading edge of the residual viable carcinoma, confirmed by D2-40 immunohistochemistry.

The posterior (SMA) inked margin is free of carcinoma at 0.1 cm clearance, with acellular fibrous tissue and scattered macrophages at the margin interface — no viable carcinoma cells are present at the inked surface. The pancreatic neck margin shows chronic pancreatitis and PanIN1 without carcinoma. Remaining margins are widely negative.

All 22 lymph nodes are negative for metastatic carcinoma. Two peripancreatic nodes demonstrate residual fibrous nodules with hemosiderin, consistent with complete regression of prior nodal metastases.

Background pancreas shows diffuse chronic obstructive pancreatitis with acinar atrophy, ductal dilatation, periductal fibrosis, and islet cell clustering. PanIN-1 and PanIN-2 lesions are present in non-involved pancreatic ducts remote from the primary tumor. No PanIN-3 (carcinoma in situ) is identified.

The duodenal mucosa, gallbladder, common bile duct, and proximal jejunal segment show no malignancy.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY SYNOPTIC REPORT (CAP Protocol for Exocrine Pancreatic Tumors)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Procedure: Pancreaticoduodenectomy (pylorus-preserving)
Tumor site: Head and uncinate process
Tumor size: 2.1 cm (greatest dimension of residual viable tumor)
Histologic type: Ductal adenocarcinoma
Histologic grade: Moderately differentiated (G2)
Neoadjuvant therapy: Yes — modified FOLFIRINOX (8 cycles) + chemoradiation (50.4 Gy + capecitabine)
Tumor regression score (CAP): Score 2 (moderate response)
Microscopic tumor extension: Peripancreatic soft tissue (ypT2)
Margins:
   Pancreatic neck: Uninvolved (0.4 cm)
   Common bile duct: Uninvolved (0.8 cm)
   Posterior/SMA: Uninvolved (0.1 cm — close)
   Anterior surface: Uninvolved (0.3 cm)
   Uncinate process: Uninvolved (0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present (focal)
Perineural invasion: Present
Lymph nodes: 0 of 22 positive (ypN0)
pTNM (post-neoadjuvant): ypT2 ypN0
Pathologic stage: Stage IIA (AJCC 8th edition, post-neoadjuvant)
Resection: R0 (complete, microscopically negative margins)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSTIC COMMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

This pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen demonstrates a moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma with a moderate treatment response (CAP regression score 2) following neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX and chemoradiation. Residual viable carcinoma accounts for approximately 25-35% of the original tumor bed. All surgical margins are negative (R0 resection). Regional lymph nodes are negative (0/22; ypN0), with two nodes demonstrating histologic evidence of complete regression of prior nodal metastases — consistent with the observed decline in CA 19-9 and radiographic lymph node response during neoadjuvant therapy. The close posterior (SMA) margin of 0.1 cm, while negative, should be considered in the context of risk for local recurrence.

Presence of perineural invasion and focal lymphovascular invasion in residual viable tumor are adverse histopathologic features in the post-neoadjuvant setting. SMAD4 loss confirmed by IHC (concordant with prior biopsy and genomic data) and aberrant p53 pattern are consistent with the previously characterized molecular profile. MMR protein expression is intact (all four proteins retained), supporting prior MSS determination.

Findings reported to the referring surgical oncology team.

PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY — GASTROINTESTINAL/HEPATOBILIARY DIVISION
ELECTRONICALLY VERIFIED AND SIGNED

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
END OF REPORT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
